_meta.description: Loading screen quotes for COINCUBE desktop and mobile apps. Displayed below Kage animations during loading, syncing, transaction, and other app states.
_meta.usage: Serve randomly within the matching context. Short quotes for brief states; medium quotes for longer screens. Every context has at least 2 quotes.

categories.booth: Jeff Booth — Author of The Price of Tomorrow, Bitcoin advocate
categories.ammous: Saifedean Ammous — Author of The Bitcoin Standard
categories.svetski: Aleks Svetski — Author of The Bushido of Bitcoin
categories.szabo: Nick Szabo — Computer scientist, smart contracts pioneer
categories.finney: Hal Finney — Cryptographer, first Bitcoin transaction recipient
categories.satoshi: Satoshi Nakamoto — Bitcoin whitepaper, forum posts, emails (2008–2010)
categories.sovereign: James Dale Davidson & Lord William Rees-Mogg — The Sovereign Individual, 1997
categories.fuller: R. Buckminster Fuller — Inventor, futurist, systems thinker
categories.hayek: Friedrich A. Hayek — Nobel laureate, Austrian School economist
categories.mises: Ludwig von Mises — Austrian School economist, monetary theorist
categories.rothbard: Murray N. Rothbard — Austrian School economist, libertarian theorist
categories.hoppe: Hans-Hermann Hoppe — Austrian School economist, political philosopher
categories.scripture: Holy Bible, New King James Version (NKJV)

contexts.loading: Chain verification, general loading, block sync
contexts.syncing: Longer sync operations, catching up with the chain
contexts.first-launch: Onboarding, first time opening the app
contexts.transaction-sent: After a send transaction is broadcast
contexts.backup-reminder: Seed phrase backup, key management prompts
contexts.error: Failed transaction, network error, blocked path
contexts.balance-update: Balance has changed, new data loaded

quotes.text: Exponentially advancing technological gains bring efficiency. That efficiency is deflationary.
quotes.author: Jeff Booth
quotes.source: The Greatest Game, The Bitcoin Times Ed. 3, 2020

quotes.text: Inflation is a tax on those most unable to pay.
quotes.author: Jeff Booth
quotes.source: The Greatest Game, The Bitcoin Times Ed. 3, 2020

quotes.text: For the first time in history… the unique characteristics of Bitcoin give everyone a choice on how they play the game.
quotes.author: Jeff Booth
quotes.source: The Greatest Game, The Bitcoin Times Ed. 3, 2020

quotes.text: Learn about Bitcoin. Learn why its importance is so much greater than the wealth it might create for you and your family.
quotes.author: Jeff Booth
quotes.source: The Greatest Game, The Bitcoin Times Ed. 3, 2020

quotes.text: Bitcoin is the hardest money ever invented: growth in its value cannot possibly increase its supply.
quotes.author: Saifedean Ammous

quotes.text: Bitcoin can be best understood as distributed software that allows for transfer of value using a currency protected from unexpected inflation without relying on trusted third parties.
quotes.author: Saifedean Ammous

quotes.text: The Samurai believed in doing, not saying. Action and behavior demonstrated their faith, knowledge and values, more than words ever could.
quotes.author: Aleks Svetski

quotes.text: There is a biological and memetic recognition that a return to paternal leadership and traditional "revolutionary" stability are what's needed to correct our bent-out-of-shape society and inverted culture. For this, patriarchs of a new caliber must rise - mature and responsible men who both respect tradition and have the courage to venture forth and establish something new.
quotes.author: Aleks Svetski

quotes.text: This period of human history will not only herald the largest wealth transfer in human history, but I'd go so far as to call it a speciation event.
quotes.author: Aleks Svetski

quotes.text: Beauty and life always win in the end, and this is why I believe that we had to go through this. We are the marble, and we are sculpting something new.
quotes.author: Aleks Svetski

quotes.text: Trusted third parties are security holes.
quotes.author: Nick Szabo
quotes.source: Trusted Third Parties Are Security Holes, 2001

quotes.text: If clams can be money, furs can be money, gold can be money, and so on – if money is not just coins or notes issued by a government under legal tender laws, but rather can be a wide variety of objects – then just what is money anyway?
quotes.author: Nick Szabo

quotes.text: Running bitcoin
quotes.author: Hal Finney

quotes.text: I believe this will be the ultimate fate of Bitcoin, to be the 'high-powered money' that serves as a reserve currency for banks that issue their own digital cash.
quotes.author: Hal Finney

quotes.text: It seemed so obvious to me: Here we are faced with the problems of loss of privacy, creeping computerization, massive databases, more centralization — and Chaum offers a completely different direction to go in, one which puts power into the hands of individuals rather than governments and corporations.
quotes.author: Hal Finney

quotes.text: The root problem with conventional currency is all the trust that's required to make it work.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: The central bank must be trusted not to debase the currency, but the history of fiat currencies is full of breaches of that trust.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: Banks must be trusted to hold our money and transfer it electronically, but they lend it out in waves of credit bubbles with barely a fraction in reserve.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: Lost coins only make everyone else's coins worth slightly more. Think of it as a donation to everyone.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: It's completely decentralized, with no central server or trusted parties, because everything is based on crypto proof instead of trust.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: The Times 03/Jan/2009 Chancellor on brink of second bailout for banks.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: If you don't believe me or don't get it, I don't have time to try to convince you, sorry.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: In a few decades when the reward gets too small, the transaction fee will become the main compensation for nodes.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: Instead of the supply changing to keep the value the same, the supply is predetermined and the value changes.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: I hope it's obvious it was only the centrally controlled nature of those systems that doomed them. I think this is the first time we're trying a decentralized, non-trust-based system.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: Coins have to get initially distributed somehow, and a constant rate seems like the best formula.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: With e-currency based on cryptographic proof, without the need to trust a third party middleman, money can be secure and transactions effortless.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: Faster than all but a few now imagine, microprocessing will subvert and destroy the nationstate, creating new forms of social organization in the process.
quotes.author: James Dale Davidson & Lord William Rees-Mogg

quotes.text: In the new millennium, cybermoney controlled by private markets will supersede fiat money issued by governments. Only the poor will be victims of inflation.
quotes.author: James Dale Davidson & Lord William Rees-Mogg

quotes.text: New technologies will allow the holders of wealth to bypass the national monopolies that have issued and regulated money in the modern period.
quotes.author: James Dale Davidson & Lord William Rees-Mogg

quotes.text: An entirely new realm of economic activity that is not hostage to physical violence will emerge in cyberspace.
quotes.author: James Dale Davidson & Lord William Rees-Mogg

quotes.text: You never change things by fighting the existing reality. To change something, build a new model that makes the existing model obsolete.
quotes.author: R. Buckminster Fuller
quotes.source: Widely attributed; cited by Jeff Booth in The Greatest Game, 2020

quotes.text: I don't believe we shall ever have a good money again before we take the thing out of the hands of government.
quotes.author: Friedrich A. Hayek
quotes.source: Interview with James U. Blanchard III, 1984

quotes.text: All we can do is by some sly roundabout way introduce something they can't stop.
quotes.author: Friedrich A. Hayek
quotes.source: Interview with James U. Blanchard III, 1984

quotes.text: I do not think it is an exaggeration to say that history is largely a history of inflation, and usually of inflations engineered for the gain of governments.
quotes.author: Friedrich A. Hayek

quotes.text: With the exception only of the 200-year period of the gold standard, practically all governments of history have used their exclusive power to issue money in order to defraud and plunder the people.
quotes.author: Friedrich A. Hayek

quotes.text: We shall not grow wiser before we learn that much that we have done was very foolish.
quotes.author: Friedrich A. Hayek

quotes.text: The curious task of economics is to demonstrate to men how little they really know about what they imagine they can design.
quotes.author: Friedrich A. Hayek

quotes.text: It is impossible to grasp the meaning of the idea of sound money if one does not realize that it was devised as an instrument for the protection of civil liberties against despotic inroads on the part of governments.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises
quotes.source: The Theory of Money and Credit, 1912

quotes.text: The gold standard alone makes the determination of money's purchasing power independent of the ambitions and machinations of governments.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: The first thing a genius needs is to breathe free air.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: A prosperity created by an expansionist monetary and credit policy is illusory and must end in a slump, an economic crisis.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises
quotes.source: Inflation Must End in a Slump, 1951

quotes.text: Inflation is not an act of God. Inflation is not a catastrophe of the elements or a disease. Inflation is a policy.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: The sound-money principle is negative in obstructing the government's propensity to meddle with the currency system.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises
quotes.source: The Theory of Money and Credit, 1912

quotes.text: Inflation is a powerful and subtle means for government acquisition of the public's resources, a painless and all the more dangerous form of taxation.
quotes.author: Murray N. Rothbard
quotes.source: What Has Government Done to Our Money?, 1963

quotes.text: It is impossible for the Federal Reserve to go bankrupt; it holds the legal monopoly of counterfeiting in the entire country.
quotes.author: Murray N. Rothbard

quotes.text: Monetary inflation not only raises prices and destroys the value of the currency unit; it also acts as a giant system of expropriation.
quotes.author: Murray N. Rothbard

quotes.text: The monopolization of money and banking is the ultimate pillar on which the modern state rests.
quotes.author: Hans-Hermann Hoppe

quotes.text: More money makes its monopolistic producer and its earliest recipients richer at the expense of making the money's late and latest receivers poorer.
quotes.author: Hans-Hermann Hoppe

quotes.text: Diverse weights and diverse measures, they are both alike, an abomination to the LORD.
quotes.author: Proverbs 20:10

quotes.text: Diverse weights are an abomination to the LORD, and dishonest scales are not good.
quotes.author: Proverbs 20:23

quotes.text: Dishonest scales are an abomination to the LORD, but a just weight is His delight.
quotes.author: Proverbs 11:1

quotes.text: You shall have honest scales, honest weights, an honest ephah, and an honest hin: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.
quotes.author: Leviticus 19:36

quotes.text: You shall not have in your bag differing weights, a heavy and a light. You shall have a perfect and just weight, a perfect and just measure, that your days may be lengthened in the land which the LORD your God is giving you.
quotes.author: Deuteronomy 25:13, 15

quotes.text: Shall I count pure those with the wicked scales, and with the bag of deceitful weights?
quotes.author: Micah 6:11

quotes.text: Your silver has become dross, your wine mixed with water.
quotes.author: Isaiah 1:22

quotes.text: Getting treasures by a lying tongue is the fleeting fantasy of those who seek death.
quotes.author: Proverbs 21:6

quotes.text: But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things shall be added to you.
quotes.author: Matthew 6:33

quotes.text: A good man leaves an inheritance to his children's children, but the wealth of the sinner is stored up for the righteous.
quotes.author: Proverbs 13:22

quotes.text: A prudent man foresees evil and hides himself, but the simple pass on and are punished.
quotes.author: Proverbs 22:3

quotes.text: There is desirable treasure, and oil in the dwelling of the wise, but a foolish man squanders it.
quotes.author: Proverbs 21:20

quotes.text: Wealth gained by dishonesty will be diminished, but he who gathers by labor will increase.
quotes.author: Proverbs 13:11

quotes.text: He who loves silver will not be satisfied with silver; nor he who loves abundance, with increase.
quotes.author: Ecclesiastes 5:10

quotes.text: The hand of the diligent makes rich.
quotes.author: Proverbs 10:4

quotes.text: A faithful man will abound with blessings, but he who hastens to be rich will not go unpunished.
quotes.author: Proverbs 28:20
